input.background: We want to test $H_0: p_1=p_2$ at $\alpha=0.05$ (two sided) with sample sizes constrained to $n_1=250$ and $n_2=300$. Educated guesses for population proportions are $p_1=0.20$ and $p_2=0.15$.
input.question: What is the power of this test?

output.answer:
\begin{aligned}
& q_1=1-p_1=1-0.20=0.80 \
& q_2=1-p_2=1-0.15=0.85 \
& \bar{p}=\frac{n_1 p_1+n_2 p_2}{n_1+n_2}=\frac{(250)(0.2)+(300)(0.15)}{(250+300)}=0.172727 \
& \bar{q}=1-\bar{p}=1-0.172727=0.827272 \
& z_{1-(\alpha / 2)}=z_{1-(.05 / 2)}=z_{.975}=1.96 \
& 1-\beta=\Phi\left(\frac{\left|p_1-p_2\right|}{\sqrt{\frac{p_1 q_1}{n_1}+\frac{p_2 q_2}{n_2}}}-\frac{z_{1-\alpha / 2} \sqrt{\bar{p}\bar{q}\left(\frac{1}{n_1}+\frac{1}{n_2}\right)}}{\sqrt{\frac{p_1 q_1}{n_1}+\frac{p_2 q_2}{n_2}}}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\Phi\left(\frac{|0.20-0.15|}{\sqrt{[(.2)(.8) / 250]+[(.15)(.85) / 300]}}\right. \
& \left.-\frac{1.96 \sqrt{(0.172727)(0.827272)\left(\frac{1}{250}+\frac{1}{300}\right)}}{\sqrt{[(.2)(.8) / 250]+[(.15)(.85) / 300]}}\right) \
& =\Phi(1.532-1.944)=\Phi(-0.41)=0.3409 \cong 34 \%
\end{aligned}
$$
